evals.name: explicit-table-list-cutover
evals.prompt: We're migrating our orders and customers tables from Snowflake (RAW.PUBLIC schema) to Databricks (catalog prod, schema sales) as part of a warehouse migration. Cutover is this weekend. Can you build an Orchestra pipeline that checks row counts and the total_amount sum on orders, plus row counts on customers, to make sure the migration landed correctly? Exact match required, no tolerance.
evals.expected_output: An Orchestra pipeline YAML using DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY tasks: a row-count check for both orders and customers, and a total_amount sum check for orders, each with source_integration SNOWFLAKE / destination_integration DATABRICKS, correctly-qualified table names, and an explicit error_threshold_expression (exact match, e.g. != 0) on every task.
assertions: Uses integration: ORCHESTRA with integration_job: DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY for the comparison tasks
Sets source_integration to SNOWFLAKE and destination_integration to DATABRICKS
Includes a row-count comparison for the orders table
Includes a row-count comparison for the customers table
Includes a total_amount sum comparison for the orders table
Every DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY task sets an explicit error_threshold_expression (never left unset)
Thresholds reflect an exact-match intent (e.g. != 0) rather than a loose tolerance, matching the user's 'no tolerance' requirement
Every source_query/destination_query resolves to a single scalar (no SELECT *, no multi-row GROUP BY)
Table names are qualified per engine convention (e.g. RAW.PUBLIC.ORDERS for Snowflake, catalog.schema.table for Databricks) rather than left as placeholders with no explanation
No hardcoded credentials or secrets appear in the YAML
The written response clearly explains what is checked and flags any assumptions made instead of silently guessing

evals.name: schema-wide-with-monitor
evals.prompt: We're replatforming from SQL Server (dbo schema, LegacyDW database) to Snowflake (ANALYTICS database, CORE schema) — basically the whole schema, dozens of tables, I don't have an exact list handy. After the initial migration validates, I also want an hourly job going forward to catch any drift using something like updated_at columns. Can you set this up?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes there's no fixed table list and either asks the user to paste the information_schema table/column list or proposes a self-discovering pipeline, rather than inventing table names. Produces (or clearly plans) two pipelines: a manual-query full validation pipeline and a scheduled (hourly cron) DATA_RECONCILIATION_CURSOR_FIELD monitor keyed on an updated_at-style column, asking which column is genuinely monotonic per table rather than assuming.
assertions: Does not invent a specific, made-up table list and present it as the real schema contents without flagging it as illustrative/placeholder

Either asks the user to supply the table/column list (e.g. via an information_schema query they run) or clearly builds a self-discovering pipeline pattern, rather than silently stalling with no path forward
Produces two distinct pipeline concepts: a one-off full validation pipeline and a separate ongoing scheduled monitor pipeline
The monitor pipeline uses integration_job DATA_RECONCILIATION_CURSOR_FIELD
The monitor pipeline has an hourly cron schedule (or explicitly notes hourly as requested)
The monitor task(s) reference an updated_at-style cursor column for both source and destination
Explicitly asks or flags whether the chosen cursor column is genuinely monotonic rather than silently assuming it is
Sets source_integration to SQL_SERVER and destination_integration to SNOWFLAKE correctly
If the self-discovery pattern is used, explains the information_schema query + set_outputs + matrix-expression mechanism coherently rather than a broken or hand-wavy version

evals.name: unsupported-engine-guardrail
evals.prompt: We just moved our Postgres analytics DB over to Snowflake and I want a reconciliation pipeline in Orchestra comparing the public.events table in Postgres against the new RAW.EVENTS table in Snowflake to make sure nothing was dropped.
evals.expected_output: Correctly flags that Orchestra's native Data Reconciliation tasks only support SNOWFLAKE, SQL_SERVER, and DATABRICKS as source/destination — Postgres isn't one of them — rather than silently generating a DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY task with an unsupported integration. Either stops to clarify, or proposes a materially different manual/fallback approach while being explicit that it isn't the native DataRec feature.
assertions: Explicitly states that Orchestra's native Data Reconciliation tasks only support SNOWFLAKE, SQL_SERVER, and DATABRICKS as source/destination
Explicitly states Postgres is not one of the supported engines for this feature
Does not produce a DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY or DATA_RECONCILIATION_CURSOR_FIELD task with POSTGRES as source_integration or destination_integration
Does not silently proceed as if the unsupported pairing would work

If a fallback approach is proposed (e.g. a plain RUN_QUERY/RUN_TEST-based manual comparison), it is clearly labeled as a different approach from the native DataRec feature, not presented as equivalent
The response stops to clarify or clearly states the limitation rather than guessing past it

evals.name: same-engine-migration
evals.prompt: We're doing a schema reorg inside our Snowflake account -- migrating our PROD database into a new MIGRATION database (same account, same warehouse). Can you build a reconciliation pipeline checking row counts on our orders and customers tables, the total_amount sum on orders, and freshness of updated_at on orders? We want exact matches on the row counts, but some tolerance is fine on the sum and timestamp checks.
evals.expected_output: A native DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY pipeline with source_integration and destination_integration both SNOWFLAKE (same engine on both sides), row-count checks for orders/customers with exact-match thresholds, a total_amount sum check and an updated_at freshness check for orders with tolerant literal-integer thresholds, no Python fallback task, and no confusion with the unsupported-engine case.
assertions: Uses integration: ORCHESTRA with integration_job: DATA_RECONCILIATION_MANUAL_QUERY for the comparison tasks
Sets both source_integration and destination_integration to SNOWFLAKE (same engine on both sides, not a different engine)
Includes a row-count comparison for the orders table
Includes a row-count comparison for the customers table
Includes a total_amount sum comparison for the orders table
Includes an updated_at freshness/max-timestamp comparison for the orders table
Row-count thresholds reflect an exact-match intent (e.g. != 0)
Sum and timestamp thresholds reflect a tolerance (not exact match), matching the user's explicit ask for some tolerance there
Every error_threshold_expression is a literal comparator + non-negative integer (no decimals, no negative numbers, no matrix-templated value)
Does not introduce a Python/PYTHON_EXECUTE_SCRIPT task for the comparison -- native DATA_RECONCILIATION tasks plus a MetaEngine/matrix are sufficient here since both sides are supported engines
Does not mention Postgres, an unsupported-engine fallback, or otherwise treat this as anything other than a straightforward same-engine reconciliation
